neighborhoods.name: Cooper-Grant / Downtown
neighborhoods.description: Rutgers-Camden, Cooper University Hospital, and the Camden waterfront across the Delaware from Philadelphia, with the Adventure Aquarium and BB&T Pavilion

housing.predominantType: University apartments, condos, and historic row homes

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Camden. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Parkside
neighborhoods.description: Forest Hill Park-adjacent neighborhood with historic detached homes, in-progress revitalization, and proximity to PATCO transit

housing.predominantType: Historic detached homes and twin houses

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Camden's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Cramer Hill
neighborhoods.description: North Camden dense residential along the Delaware and Cooper rivers with very affordable housing and ongoing waterfront redevelopment

housing.predominantType: Row homes, twins, and small detached homes

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Fairview / Yorkship Village
neighborhoods.description: Camden's southwest with the Yorkship Village historic planned community, more single-family housing, and quieter streets
neighborhoods.character_notes: Family-friendly area with good schools, parks, and community amenities. Popular with active retirees and families.
